session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8596 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute the PR feedback classifier with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by following its instructions. First, I'll fetch the PR feedback using the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command with the PR number specified.

      "body": "<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-02 01:52:28 PT

Found 6 violations across 3 files. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim code blocks (erk source) — 1 found in `land-learn-integration.md:97`
❌ Private method names in prose — Found in `git-plumbing-patterns.md:52,63` and `land-learn-integration.md:53,66`
❌ Drift risk: hardcoded constant values — Found in `workflow-run-list.md:53`

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md:52`: Private method `_save_as_planned_pr()` in prose
- `docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md:63`: Private method `_dispatch_planned_pr_plan()` with line numbers in prose
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:53`: Private method `_compute_session_stats()` with line range in prose
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:66`: Private method `_session_type_prefix()` in prose
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:97`: VERBATIM erk source code block (try/except)
- `docs/learned/cli/workflow-run-list.md:53`: Constants table with hardcoded values (enumerable catalog drift risk)

### Files Reviewed
| File | Verdict | Issues |
|------|---------|--------|
| `docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md` | KEEP | 0 |
| `docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md` | SIMPLIFY | 2 private method names in prose |
| `docs/learned/architecture/plan-save-branch-restoration.md` | DELETED | n/a |
| `docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md` | KEEP | 0 (line numbers in prose noted but not flagged) |
| `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md` | SIMPLIFY | 3 violations (2 private methods, 1 verbatim block) |
| `docs/learned/cli/workflow-run-list.md` | SIMPLIFY | 1 drift risk (constants table) |
| `docs/learned/tui/keyboard-shortcuts.md` | KEEP | 0 |
| `docs/learned/tui/tui-command-registration.md` | KEEP | 0 (launch key table is high-value reference cache) |

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-02 01:52:28 PT**: Found 6 violations across 3 files: 3 private method names in prose, 1 verbatim code block, 1 constants drift risk in new docs (git-plumbing-patterns.md, land-learn-integration.md, workflow-run-list.md)

</details>",
      "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8596#issuecomment-3983301092"
    }
  ]
}

Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me classify the feedback according to the Comment Classification Model in the skill.
Based on the PR feedback data:
1. **Review threads**: None (empty array) 2. **Discussion comments**: - Comment 3983209897 (schrockn): "Plan Queued for Implementation" - This is a machine-generated metadata block from the erk system - Comment 3983212537 (github-actions[bot]): "GitHub Action Started" - This is a workflow status update from the system - Comment 3983301092 (github-actions[bot]): "Audit PR Docs" - This is a bot suggestion for documentation improvements
**Classification Analysis**:
- Comment 3983209897: Informational (machine-generated metadata, system acknowledgment) - Comment 3983212537: Informational (CI/workflow status update) - Comment 3983301092: Actionable (bot suggestion to fix documentation violations - "simplify" recommendations for 3 files)
**Pre-Existing Detection**: - The git diff shows that documentation files were modified/created as part of this PR (new files, modifications to existing docs) - The audit bot is flagging issues in those same documentation files that were changed in this PR - These are not pre-existing issues—they're directly caused by the documentation additions/changes in this PR itself
**Complexity for Actionable Issues**: - The audit identifies issues across 3 files (cross-cutting): `git-plumbing-patterns.md`, `land-learn-integration.md`, `workflow-run-list.md`
